{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the provided documents, nociceptive signals are primarily transmitted via the spinothalamic tract to the thalamus and then to the somatosensory cortex. The documents mention that nociceptive information from the face and intraoral structures is transmitted via first-order neurons in the trigeminal ganglia, which project to the pons and spinal trigeminal nucleus in the medulla before synapsing with neurons in the ventral posteromedial nucleus (VPM). The ventral posteromedial nucleus is part of the thalamus. The reticular system is involved in pain modulation but not as the primary target for nociceptive signals. The nucleus caudalis is part of the medulla oblongata and is not specifically mentioned as a primary target for nociceptive signals. The superior cervical ganglion is part of the autonomic nervous system and not directly involved in nociceptive signaling to the brain.", "answer_choice": "B"}
